en: I had it all. I had a stereo that was very decent,
ko: 나는 모든 것을 가졌었다. 꽤 괜찮은 스테레오가 있었고,

en: a wardrobe that was getting very respectable.
I was close to being complete.
Shit, man. Now it's all gone.
It's all gone.
All gone.
Do you know what a duvet is? Comfort.
It's a blanket. Just a blanket.
Why do guys like you and I know what a duvet is?
ko: 왜 너와 내가 이불이 뭔지 알까?

en: Is this essential to our survival
in the hunter-gatherer sense of the word?
No.
What are we, then?
Well, you know, consumers.
Right. We are consumers.
We are byproducts of a lifestyle obsession.
Murder, crime, poverty, these things don't concern me.
ko: 살인, 범죄, 빈곤, 이런 건 나와 상관없어.

en: What concerns me are celebrity magazines,
television with 500 channels,
some guy's name on my underwear,
ko: 내 속옷에 적힌 어떤 남자의 이름,

en: Rogaine, Viagra, Olestra.
Martha Stewart.
Fuck Martha Stewart.
Arthur's Polishing Nebraska.
